title: Bard — Channel Invite / one-click redemption (Sprint B3, feature #67/#69)
description: FROZEN 2026-06-12; upgraded 2026-06-18 (ADR-0016 / Step S3) to ASYMMETRIC device identity. The "send a link, click, you're in" flow: an owner/manager mints a shareable INVITE for a channel; a worker opens the link/QR and redeems it, presenting the device's Ed25519 public key; the device is admitted ACTIVE into that channel in ONE step — no manual approve. This is deliberately DISTINCT from the fleet enrollment lifecycle in enrollment.schema.json: fleet enrollment is join-token -> pending -> (manager approve) -> active, because the fleet operator vets each node; an invite is the owner PRE-authorizing membership by the act of sending the link, so redemption skips the approve gate. An invite token is single-use (consumed on first successful redeem) and expires; a consumed/expired/unknown invite MUST fail redemption. The invite token is a CHANNEL credential (audience bard-channel-invite, carries the channelId), never a per-device credential and never a fleet join token (different audience). There is NO server-minted deviceSecret: the device self-signs EdDSA tokens with the private key it never discloses, so the redeem response returns the device record (with its registered public key) and the channelId, never a secret.

ChannelId.description: Stable identifier for a channel / group (e.g. a Squawk Box walkie-talkie channel). Carried in the invite token (claim 'cid') and in channel-membership records.

InviteToken.description: Shareable, single-use, expiring bearer embedded in the invite URL/QR. Audience bard-channel-invite; carries the channelId (cid) and a unique id (jti) that the store burns on first successful redeem. NOT a device credential and NOT a fleet join token.

InviteRecord.description: The authoritative invite record. No token signing material is stored here; the token verifies against the channel-invite signing secret held server-side.

inviteId.description: Unique invite id (equals the token 'jti'); the single-use key the store burns on redeem.

label.description: Operator-supplied human label for the invite (a writer-facing name, e.g. 'North site crew').

expiresAt.description: When the invite token stops being redeemable.

redeemed.description: True once consumed; a redeemed invite cannot be redeemed again (single-use).

redeemedAt.description: When the invite was redeemed, or null while unredeemed.

redeemedBy.description: The deviceId that redeemed the invite, or null while unredeemed.

CreateInviteRequest.description: POST /invites body: a manager creates an invite for a channel.

ttlSeconds.description: Optional override for the invite lifetime; defaults to the server's configured invite TTL when omitted.

CreateInviteResponse.description: POST /invites 200: the new invite record, the shareable token, and a ready-to-send URL embedding it. QR rendering is client-side (the URL is the QR payload); the server does not render an image.

inviteUrl.description: The link an owner sends over email/text; opening it drives the client redemption. The invite token is carried as the 'invite' query parameter.

RedeemRequest.description: POST /invites/{token}/redeem body: the redeeming device identifies itself and presents the Ed25519 public key it generated on-device. The invite token is the path segment (it is the channel authorization); no manager bearer is required because the owner pre-authorized by sending the link. The server stores only the public key.

deviceId.description: Stable identifier the redeeming device chooses for itself; becomes the per-device token 'sub'.

RedeemResponse.description: POST /invites/{token}/redeem 200: the device is admitted ACTIVE (no approve step) and joined to the channel in one call. The device record carries the registered public key; no secret is returned — the device self-signs EdDSA tokens with the private key it never disclosed.

ChannelMembership.description: Which devices belong to a channel. GET /channels/{channelId}/members projection.
